exemplar.title: Creating Workshops to Teach Medical Emergency Skills

detailedExemplarData.title: Creating Workshops to Teach Medical Emergency Skills

strengths: Rigorous research demonstrated by the CARRDS evaluation sample, ensuring content credibility
Effective self-management evidenced by planner screenshots and a quantified weekly schedule
Insightful self-assessment table tracking growth in ATL skills over time
Creative thinking showcased in the mind-map diagram and scenario cards for instructional design
Use of TikTok analytics to provide concrete evidence of product reach and engagement
Critical reflection on creative thinking limitations leading to adaptive improvements
weaknesses: Description of self-management lacks quantitative metrics to illustrate its direct impact on task completion and project progress
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The Applying Skills section offers compelling evidence of diverse ATL skillsâfrom research and organization to creative thinkingâsupported by clear visuals and analytics. Incorporating specific metrics for self-management (such as planned versus completed task ratios) would make the evidence of effectiveness more convincing.

strengths: Concise and relevant product goal focusing on hands-on workshops
Detailed and measurable success criteria covering sources, workshops, feedback ratios, and quiz improvement
Clear and feasible action plan with a sequenced timeline, defined tasks, and linked ATL skills
weaknesses: Learning goal lacks specific, measurable benchmarks to make progress quantifiable
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning section excels in defining a focused product goal, establishing a robust framework of measurable success criteria, and outlining a realistic, well-sequenced action plan. To elevate it further, the learning goal should include concrete benchmarks (for example, quiz accuracy targets) that align directly with success criteria.

strengths: Thorough evaluation of product outcomes with participant numbers and a quantified 208% quiz improvement
Reflective insight into a shift from rote memorization to deep understanding and its link to academic performance
Strong contextual awareness demonstrated through discussion of fairness and development implications
Critical self-assessment of sources and responsiveness to feedback, evidenced by a 10:1 positive-to-negative feedback ratio
Diversified impact metrics including TikTok engagement and workshop feedback
weaknesses: Could increase post-workshop engagement by integrating QR codes linking to supplementary resources
Scope of first-aid content could be broadened by covering additional scenarios (e.g., burns, concussions) to deepen learner preparedness
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflection is comprehensive, using multiple forms of evidence to assess both product effectiveness and personal learning growth while demonstrating critical self-awareness. To deepen impact, consider embedding interactive follow-up resources and expanding the range of emergency scenarios covered.

annotations.comment: The CARRDS evaluation sample shows rigorous research skills. Your analysis of credibility, accuracy, and sources underpins the reliability of workshop content.
You met your goal of three workshops with 60 participants. Reflecting on future expansion demonstrates forward-thinking in product reach and community impact.

Your learning goal is clear but could be more measurable. Consider specifying concrete benchmarks for understanding (e.g., passing a quiz with 80% accuracy) to ensure progress is quantifiable and aligned with success criteria.
Your product goal is concise and relevant. Conducting workshops leverages hands-on learning effectively to maximize audience retention of emergency procedures.

The success criteria table is detailed and measurable, covering multiple dimensions (sources, workshops, feedback ratio, quiz improvement). This provides a strong framework for evaluating your projectâs outcomes.
Your action plan presents a clear timeline, tasks, and linked ATL skills. Sequencing research, creation, and feedback demonstrates strong planning and feasibility.

The description of self-management skill is insightful yet lacks concrete evidence of effectiveness. Include metrics (e.g., completed tasks vs. planned) to illustrate how self-management improved research and project progress.
The planner screenshot provides strong visual evidence of your self-management strategies. It clearly supports how you scheduled research and practice sessions.
Your weekly schedule table quantifies your commitment (hours per week), demonstrating effective use of affective and organizational ATL skills.
Including a self-assessment table (before, during, after) highlights your growth in ATL skills. This reflective comparison strengthens the Applying Skills criterion.
The mind-map diagram visually demonstrates creative thinking and instructional design. It effectively translates complex procedures into an accessible workshop outline.
TikTok analytics (views, likes, comments) provide concrete evidence of product reach and engagement, reflecting the impact of your applied thinking skills.
Scenario cards promote active participation and peer learning. This hands-on approach exemplifies successful transfer of thinking skills into your product.
Your reflection on creative thinking limitations and subsequent adjustments demonstrates critical thinking and adaptability in workshop delivery.
You thoughtfully reflect on how your mindset shifted from memorization to deep understanding. Linking this to improved academic performance shows genuine impact on learning.
Your discussion of the Fairness and Development context is insightful. You demonstrate clear awareness of educational equity and the real-world relevance of your project.
Your evaluation confirms you exceeded the source target with 55 credible references. Acknowledging finer gaps (e.g., less detailed fire extinguisher notes) shows critical self-assessment.
A 10:1 positive-to-negative feedback ratio is exemplary. You acted on constructive criticism by deepening your Heimlich explanation, showing responsiveness to participant input.
Quantifying quiz improvement (208% increase) convincingly demonstrates that participants not only absorbed but retained the material you taught.
Posting 10 TikTok videos and analyzing engagement metrics shows strong product diversification and effective use of digital media to reinforce learning.
You met your poster prerequisite but could increase impact by integrating a QR code linking to revision resources. This would extend engagement beyond static displays.
Discussing 13 safety procedures meets expectations. To deepen learner preparedness, aim to exceed by covering additional scenarios (e.g., burns, concussions) to broaden first-aid knowledge.
